div1.entryFree: Spice, subst. an aromatic vegetable substance used for seasoning: Wint. IV, 3, 128. Plur. --s: Merch. I, 1, 33. Wint. IV, 3, 125. Rom. IV, 4, 1. Per. III, 1, 66. III, 2, 66. Figuratively: thy by-gone fooleries were but --s of it, Wint. III, 2, 185 (served only to season it, to give it a zest). for all this s. of your hypocrisy, H8 II, 3, 26 (taste, tincture). the s. and salt that season a man, Troil. I, 2, 277. he hath --s of them all, not all, Cor. IV, 7, 46.
